dimensions.name: Requirements Met
dimensions.gaps_to_10: This task (200) is an integration verification task — its only deliverable is a report confirming prior fixes are in place. All 7 verification checks are confirmed by this review: (1) build passes (exit 0, 13 pages); (2) type-check passes (tsc --noEmit exit 0, zero errors); (3) all 6 timer-bearing files use ref-based cleanup; (4) all 7 secondary buttons have min-h-[88px]; (5) dev port is 3042; (6) 212 tests across 25 suites pass; (7) all 9 component files are under 200 lines. No gaps.

dimensions.name: Error Handling
dimensions.gaps_to_10: All timer ref patterns guard against double-fire with clearTimeout-before-set. Async DB paths (parent/page.tsx, ChangePinSection, settings/page.tsx) are wrapped in try/catch with console.error logging. The one minor pre-existing gap: handleSaveProfile in parent/settings/page.tsx catches the error and logs it but does not surface any user-visible feedback on save failure, leaving the user unaware. This is a pre-existing issue out of task-200 scope and does not regress anything introduced in this branch.

dimensions.name: UX Completeness
dimensions.gaps_to_10: All 7 specified buttons have min-h-[88px] confirmed by direct file inspection. Modal buttons (Cancel + Confirm Reset in parent/page.tsx, Cancel + Confirm in settings/page.tsx) are correctly sized. Activity buttons (SongPicker Back, SongPlayerControls Retry, SongPlayerControls Back, ListenAndFind Play Again) all carry min-h-[88px]. No regressions introduced. Bilingual labels (EN/ZH) are consistent.

dimensions.name: Code Quality
dimensions.gaps_to_10: The branch introduces only documentation files (.af/tasks/200/) — no production code was changed. All inspected production files are well-structured, under 200 lines, follow consistent React patterns (useRef cleanup, async/await with try/catch, consistent Tailwind class naming). One minor pre-existing concern not introduced by this branch: the MascotIdle blinkTimer is a local variable inside setInterval rather than a ref, which means it cannot be cleaned up in a separate useEffect — however the cleanup IS co-located inside the same useEffect return function, so there is no actual leak. A cosmetic suggestion only.

dimensions.name: Test Coverage
dimensions.gaps_to_10: 25 test suites, 212 tests, all passing. Coverage spans PinPad (submit, backspace), ChangePinSection (verify flow, save error), ParentPin (wrong attempts, forgot modal), ParentSettings (save, reset confirm), MascotIdle (render, size, blink, click), ListenAndFind (correct/incorrect tap sequences), SongTime (full component), and more. No unmount-during-active-timer edge cases are unit-tested (a known gap acknowledged in prior reviews), but this is not a regression introduced in this branch and the real-world risk is handled by ref-based cleanup in production code.

dimensions.name: Security
dimensions.gaps_to_10: No hardcoded API keys, secrets, tokens, or credentials found in any source file. The grep search across all .ts and .tsx files returned no matches for apiKey, api_key, SECRET, PASSWORD, or similar patterns. No new data flows introduced. PIN verification uses crypto hashing (verifyPIN/hashPIN via Web Crypto). All DB writes are local IndexedDB. No external network calls in the modified files.

summary: Task 200 is an integration verification task with no production code changes — its only commits are three .af documentation files. All 7 verification checks have been independently confirmed by this review: npm run build exits 0 with 13 static pages, tsc --noEmit exits 0 with zero type errors, npm test passes 212 tests across 25 suites, all 6 timer-bearing files use useRef-based clearTimeout/clearInterval cleanup, all 7 secondary buttons carry min-h-[88px], the dev server is on port 3042, and all 9 component files are under 200 lines. No hardcoded secrets were found. No P1 or P2 issues were identified. Verdict: PASS.
